findings.description: The HTML includes fabricated text not present in the PDF: a Google Fonts @import URL and stylesheet/theme branding.
findings.evidence: HTML head contains "/* Digital Commons NC — Standalone Theme CSS */" and "@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Source+Sans+3...')" plus CSS variables and styling. The PDF pages only show the report content and do not contain this text or URL.

findings.description: The PDF's repeated page header metadata and page numbers are not represented in the table structure, and the page-wise split table is merged into one HTML table. This is acceptable structurally, but one table label row is altered/incomplete.
findings.evidence:
PDF page 1 shows section total row labeled "Total Cost For" under "A. Division of Social Services" with the note below; HTML preserves this as a combined cell "Total Cost For
Note that the totals may be off due to rounding." However, for the grand total the PDF shows "Grand Total" and "All Programs and Administration" as a grouped label across the bottom, while HTML separates them into two rows, one blank-valued row "Grand Total" and one data row "All Programs and Administration".

findings.description: Heading hierarchy is not faithfully represented. Most PDF section headings are embedded as plain table rows instead of semantic headings, leaving only one h1 in HTML.
findings.evidence:
PDF has visible section headings such as "A. Division of Social Services", "1. Public Assistance Programs", "2. Public Assistance Administration", and "3. Services Programs". HTML uses only "

Stanly County

" and places all other headings as regular entries inside the table.
